channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: GTA VI + GPT-6: Rockstar's AI Mechanics Revealed
item.description: AI Master: A YouTube video claims to reveal Rockstar's actual AI architecture for GTA VI through patent analysis and leaked documents, debunking viral false claims that it uses GPT-6. The creator argues Rockstar has built superior real-time behavioral simulation systems for persistent NPC memory and dynamic mechanics, positioning this as a gaming AI milestone distinct from large language models.

item.title: What's new with data agents
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech presented updates to data analytics agents with new capabilities for data engineering, science, and conversational analytics. The session covered the Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Model Context Protocol (MCP) integrations, accessible through Gemini CLI, Claude Code, Codex, and IDEs like VSCode.

item.title: Production Evals For Agentic AI Systems - Nishant Gupta, Meta Superintelligence Labs
item.description: AI Engineer: Nishant Gupta from Meta SuperIntelligence Labs presents evaluation frameworks for autonomous AI agents in production. The talk covers limitations of offline benchmarks, continuous evaluation pipelines, detection of drift and hallucinations, and metrics for multi-step agentic workflows.

item.title: Build Systems, Not Code - Angie Jones, Agentic AI Foundation
item.description: AI Engineer: Angie Jones argues that AI coding agents shift software engineering work from line-by-line code writing to system-level design. She contends that core engineering disciplines like systems thinking, decomposition, and state management remain essential, with engineering value moving to the architectural layer rather than disappearing.

item.title: Anthropic's Labs Lead On Fable's Capabilities + Building AI-Native Products — With Mike Krieger
item.description: Alex Kantrowitz: Mike Krieger, head of Anthropic Labs and Instagram co-founder, discusses Fable's capabilities and AI-native product development in a podcast interview. He describes how working with Fable changes the development workflow and explains Anthropic's safety warnings as substantive rather than marketing-driven. The episode covers Anthropic's dual role as both a platform and product company.

item.title: Power intelligent agents with AI-native databases
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud speakers argue that AI-native databases are critical infrastructure for agent systems, enabling context-aware automation through retrieval-augmented generation and multimodal interfaces. They highlight open standards like Model Context Protocol and agentic IDEs as key enablers for rapid, reliable agent deployment.

item.title: Boost AI context with hybrid search in Spanner
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Spanner now combines full-text and vector search in multi-model queries for AI-powered contextual search and retrieval-augmented generation applications. The platform integrates with Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform to enable sophisticated AI-powered experiences with operational data.

item.title: Agent development and AgentOps with BigQuery, ADK, and MCP
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud presented a session on Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration for standardized agent development. The session covered building agents that access BigQuery, Google Maps, and AlloyDB through unified connectors, plus implementing AgentOps for observability and cost monitoring.

item.title: Build connected AI: Orchestrate tools and agents with registries and ADK
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud's Agent Development Kit (ADK) with Agent Registry enables developers to build connected agents that orchestrate tools and access organizational data across SaaS and on-premises systems. The framework integrates with Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers and includes specification boosters to transform APIs into agent-ready tools.

item.title: What's new with Gemini from Google DeepMind
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google DeepMind announced Gemini 3, featuring upgraded model reasoning, expanded context window processing, and enhanced multimodal tool use. The model is designed for building agentic applications and is integrated with Vertex AI for deployment and scaling.

item.title: Building an AI app: A low-code guide for small teams
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud offers a low-code approach to building AI applications using Gemini, Cloud SQL, and AlloyDB for PostgreSQL. The platform enables small teams to implement natural language interfaces, hybrid search, image search, and recommendations through auto-generated code without requiring specialized AI expertise.

item.title: What's new in Looker: Empowering business users in the governed agentic era
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud announced advancements to Looker, its business intelligence platform, including dashboard agents that automate workflows and insights, shared pre-trained agents, and Conversational Analytics. The system grounds AI in a governed semantic layer to enable agentic capabilities while maintaining data governance and consistency.

item.title: NoSQL for modern apps and AI: The future of Memorystore, Firestore, and Bigtable
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud presented the latest advancements in its NoSQL database offerings: Memorystore, Firestore, and Bigtable. The session covered new product releases, architectural innovations, and roadmaps designed to support AI applications and agentic workloads with high performance, flexibility, and scalability.

item.title: Implementing DeepMind innovation: Deep research API
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google DeepMind has developed a Deep research API that enables agents powered by Gemini to perform comprehensive research tasks with improved performance. The API can be implemented in applications using Google Cloud services.

item.title: What's new in Cloud Run
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech announces new Cloud Run capabilities for deploying AI agents and inference workloads with serverless features. The session covers scaling applications, reducing operational overhead, and addressing next-generation AI-driven development needs.

item.title: What's new in Cloud SQL: Drive performance, high availability, and security
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud SQL supports MySQL, PostgreSQL, and SQL Server with new AI-driven capabilities for automated health management and fleet-wide insights. The service emphasizes performance, high availability, security, and facilitates database migration to the cloud through Database Migration Service.

item.title: What's new in Google Cloud's agent platform
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud unveiled an enhanced agent platform featuring a unified Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform Agent Builder with no-code/low-code design capabilities. The platform includes an Agent Registry and marketplace for component distribution, enterprise governance tools, observability with time-travel debugging, evaluation suites, and a managed simulation environment. Additional features encompass an expanded Agent Development Kit (ADK) with broader language support, native agent-to-agent commun

item.title: The Miranda Hypothesis: How Hamilton Poisoned Persona Evals - Jacob E. Thomas, Results Gen
item.description: AI Engineer: Jacob E. Thomas presents the Miranda Hypothesis, which identifies how cultural representation density in training data causes persona-based AI systems to output smoothed cultural composites rather than reasoning from primary sources. He proposes a shift from cognitive simulation benchmarks to epistemic simulation with temporally-anchored, expert-evaluated rubrics that detect anachronistic reasoning and documentary inconsistencies that automated metrics miss.

item.title: What's new for AI on GKE: Training, serving, and agents
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) has added new capabilities for AI workloads including training, serving, reinforcement learning, and agentic workflows. The platform is designed to improve reliability, scalability, and cost-effectiveness for AI infrastructure that is shifting from frontier model serving to complex agentic systems.

item.title: Engineering the future of Kubernetes for AI at scale
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech presented at Google Cloud Next on evolving Kubernetes infrastructure for AI-scale workloads including agents, inference, and batch processing. The session discussed hardware-aware platforms, resource optimization, and securing agentic workflows as enterprises shift primary workloads to AI.

item.title: Unsure how to approach CS50? Carter Zenke answers.
item.description: CS50: CS50 is Harvard University's introduction to computer science and programming, offered through edX, Harvard Extension School, and Harvard Summer School. The course provides foundational instruction in computational thinking and is distributed openly via OpenCourseWare with community engagement through multiple social platforms.

item.title: Kimi K2.7 Code: THIS is VERY CRAZY MAN!
item.description: AICodeKing: Moonshot AI released Kimi K2.7 Code, an open-source agentic model for coding tasks built on a Mixture-of-Experts architecture with one trillion parameters. The model supports a 256K context window and ships with a CLI tool for repository exploration, file editing, and test execution, plus a subscription plan with refreshed weekly quota.

item.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 3 Livestream
item.description: AI Engineer: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 held its final day of main-stage programming on July 2, 2026 at Moscone West in San Francisco. The livestream featured keynotes, talks, and a startup battlefield competition, serving as a major industry gathering point.

item.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 2 Livestream
item.description: AI Engineer: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 is a conference held at Moscone West in San Francisco on July 1, 2026. The event features keynote sessions and main-stage programming throughout the day, livestreamed online.

item.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 1
item.description: AI Engineer: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 is a major annual conference held June 30, 2026 at Moscone West in San Francisco. The event features keynote sessions and main-stage programming across multiple time blocks, streamed online for broader audience reach.

item.title: Anthropic accuses Alibaba of illicitly extracting AI capabilities - BBC
item.description: Google News AI: Anthropic has accused Alibaba of illicitly extracting AI capabilities, according to a BBC report. The accusation suggests unauthorized access to or replication of AI model functionality.

item.title: Ask HN: Where is our profession (programmer) going?
item.description: syntaxbush: A programmer observes that at a visited software company, LLMs like Claude have become the primary tool for code generation and problem-solving, with human code review, abstraction practices, and deep problem understanding being de-emphasized. The author expresses concern that this represents a shift from precise, knowledge-intensive work toward probabilistic, outsourced decision-making.

item.title: The Agentic OS Setup That Will 10x Claude Code
item.description: Chase AI: Chase AI presents the case that learning Agentic OS fundamentals, covering skills, loop engineering, and memory management, enhances Claude Code mastery and enables customization, visual interfaces, and shareable packages for non-technical teams.

item.title: [AINews] It's Meta-Harness Summer
item.description: Latent Space: Latent Space discusses a shift toward Meta's harness approaches for evaluating AI systems, suggesting Meta-based testing harnesses are becoming the dominant standard in the industry. The piece presents this as a significant trend shift in AI evaluation methodology.

item.title: Bible as RAG Database
item.description: jacksonastone: A discussion or exploration of using the Bible as a retrieval-augmented generation database. This applies RAG techniques to religious text retrieval and reference systems.

item.title: What I'm Finding About LLM Code Style and Token Costs
item.description: jimmont: The author reports observations about how different code styles affect token consumption when using LLMs. This is a practical analysis of code formatting patterns and their impact on inference cost, based on experimental findings.

item.title: Europe is pushing back on Washington’s chip war
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Europe is resisting U.S. export restrictions on semiconductor manufacturing equipment as part of Washington's broader strategy to contain Chinese chip capabilities. ASML CEO notes that current Chinese access is limited to older deep ultraviolet lithography tools from a decade ago, which the MATCH Act would further restrict.

item.title: We'll fight the platform war against Big AI
item.description: aendruk: An author signals intent to challenge the dominance of large AI platforms and incumbent companies. The framing suggests a platform competition dynamic between centralized AI incumbents and alternative approaches.

item.title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
item.description: Cole Medin: Cole Medin reviews Google's 50-page whitepaper on agentic engineering, arguing that model selection contributes only 10% to AI coding success while the harness (context, tools, guardrails, verification) constitutes the other 90%. The video breaks down the spectrum from vibe coding to agentic engineering and contends that context engineering and orchestration skills are the key differentiators for 2026.

item.title: simonw/browser-compat-db
item.description: Simon Willison: Simon Willison created simonw/browser-compat-db, a SQLite database conversion of Mozilla's browser compatibility data. The project includes Claude-generated Python scripts for data transformation, a GitHub Actions workflow built with GPT-4.5, and makes the database publicly accessible via GitHub CDN with CORS support for exploration via Datasette Lite.

item.title: What we learned from 1,604 Chinese AI job postings
item.description: Epoch AI: Epoch AI analyzed 1,604 Chinese AI job postings to infer the research strategies and priorities of Chinese AI labs. The analysis uses hiring patterns as a window into organizational focus areas and technology direction.

item.title: Former Infosys chief has a new startup that wants to challenge the IT services world
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Former Infosys CEO Vishal Sikka has launched a new startup backed by Mayfield and Aramco Ventures. The company brings together veterans from SAP, Infosys, and VianAI to challenge the traditional IT services sector.

item.title: Anthropic Just Replaced Claude Code With New Claude Tag
item.description: AI Revolution: Anthropic launched Claude Tag, a team-focused version of Claude Code that integrates directly into Slack, connects to company tools, and enables task management in shared channels. It replaces the previous Claude Slack chatbot for enterprise team workflows.

item.title: Omnigent: Open-source meta harness for agents
item.description: chatmasta: Omnigent is an open-source framework that provides a meta harness for agents. It appears designed to standardize or simplify the development and deployment of AI agent systems.

item.title: Cerebras stock plunges after earnings as CEO says margin outlook was misunderstood
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Cerebras, an AI chipmaker, reported lower-than-expected gross margins in its first earnings as a public company, causing its stock price to decline. The CEO attributed the market reaction to a misunderstanding of the company's margin outlook.

item.title: A24 Knows You're Mad About the Google AI Collab
item.description: nate: A24 is facing backlash from its audience over a collaboration with Google AI. The announcement has generated negative public sentiment regarding the entertainment company's partnership with the tech giant.

item.title: AI was supposed to kill engineering jobs, but new data suggests they’re the most resilient
item.description: TechCrunch AI: TechCrunch reports on SignalFire data showing that engineering jobs are resilient despite predictions of AI-driven displacement. Engineers represent a larger share of new hires in AI-affected sectors, contradicting narratives about widespread job elimination.

item.title: AI Data Center Power Requirements in 2026: The Complete Grid-to-Chip Guide
item.description: lopespm: lopespm presents an analysis of projected AI data center power consumption through 2026, covering infrastructure scaling from grid to chip-level systems. The piece examines power requirements as AI workloads expand.

item.title: AI researchers continue to leave Google for its rivals
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Top AI researchers Jonas Adler and Alexander Pritzel are departing Google to join Anthropic, continuing a trend of high-profile scientist exits from Google that previously included Noam Shazeer and John Jumper. This reflects competitive pressure in hiring and retaining AI talent.

item.title: OpenAI Codex bombards SSDs with needless write operations
item.description: jgalt212: OpenAI Codex generates excessive write operations to SSDs, potentially damaging storage hardware through unnecessary disk activity. The report highlights inefficient I/O behavior in the model's execution pipeline.

item.title: How to Automate Content Creation With AI in 2026 (AI Content Repurposing)
item.description: AI Master: Trupeer AI automates content creation from screen recordings, generating multiple assets including polished videos, documentation, and translations in minutes. The platform includes a Chrome extension, custom AI avatar creation, voice cloning, and one-click translation features.

item.title: Anthropic says Alibaba illicitly extracted Claude AI model capabilities
item.description: htrp: Anthropic publicly alleges that Alibaba has illicitly extracted capabilities from Claude AI model. This is a claim about unauthorized model distillation or extraction of proprietary model behavior.

item.title: Claude 5.1 Confirmed: What Happens After The Fable 5 Ban?
item.description: AI Master: A YouTube video claims Claude 5 (called Fable) was banned by the U.S. government 76 hours after launch due to advanced capabilities, citing benchmark scores and alleged covert throttling. The video analyzes competitive standing against GPT-5.5, Gemini, and DeepSeek while discussing regulatory risk and leverage theory around Altman and OpenAI.

item.title: Why the Frontier Ecosystem must be Open — Matei Zaharia and Reynold Xin, Databricks
item.description: Latent Space: Matei Zaharia and Reynold Xin from Databricks argue that the frontier AI ecosystem must be open for companies to build agent clouds at scale. The discussion frames openness as a prerequisite for broader adoption of AI agent infrastructure.

item.title: The Patron Saint of Murderers - Ada Palmer
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: A YouTube podcast episode from Dwarkesh Patel's channel featuring author and historian Ada Palmer discussing intellectual history, ethics, and potentially contentious historical ideas.
